div.h2: x86_64 struct user_regs_struct — Register Layout and Syscall ABI

g.text: 0x38 | 56 | unsigned long long | r10 ← ARG 4 (NOT rcx!)
0x50 | 80 | unsigned long long | rax ← RETURN VALUE (exit stop)
0x58 | 88 | unsigned long long | rcx ← CLOBBERED by syscall instr
0x78 | 120 | unsigned long long | orig_rax ← SYSCALL NR (preserved!)
Entry Stop vs Exit Stop — What Each Register Means
Error Return Convention — Kernel to Tracer
C Function ABI vs Syscall ABI
Register State Across the Entry/Exit Boundary
Register | Entry Stop (before kernel runs) | Exit Stop (after kernel runs)
orig_rax | syscall number N | syscall number N (preserved!)
rax | syscall number N (same as above)| return value (or -errno)
rcx | CLOBBERED (saved RIP by CPU) | CLOBBERED
r11 | CLOBBERED (saved RFLAGS by CPU) | CLOBBERED
CRITICAL: read arg registers at ENTRY only. Read rax (retval) at EXIT only.
Raw kernel retval in rax (at exit stop)
glibc wrapper: if (rax in [-4095,-1]) { errno=-rax; return -1; }
RULE: always use orig_rax to ID syscall
C function arg4 → rcx (System V AMD64)
C function : rdi rsi rdx rcx r8 r9
Syscall ABI : rdi rsi rdx r10 r8 r9
